name: refac-09-config-consolidation
initialPrompt: We have configuration spread across 6 different places: .env files, AWS SSM Parameter Store, Kubernetes ConfigMaps, a legacy XML config file, hardcoded constants in the codebase, and a homegrown config service from 2018. Every new engineer spends their first week just finding where a setting lives. I need: a single source of truth for all config, a migration plan that doesn't break existing deployments, and a way to detect when anyone adds a new hardcoded constant.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: Config consolidation: 6 sources → single source of truth

blocks.text: Consolidate all configuration into a single source of truth without breaking existing deployments
successCriteria: All config accessible from one location per environment
No hardcoded constants remain in source
New engineer onboarding config step takes under 15 minutes

blocks.title: Audit all 6 config sources and catalogue every key
acceptance: Master list of all config keys with source and consumers
Duplicate and conflicting keys identified

blocks.title: Design unified config schema with validation and typed access
acceptance: Config schema defined with Zod or similar
All keys typed; missing keys caught at startup

blocks.title: Migrate all 6 sources into unified store incrementally
acceptance: Each source migrated in a separate PR
Zero downtime; old and new paths parallel during migration

blocks.title: Add lint rule to detect new hardcoded config constants
acceptance: CI fails on new hardcoded URLs, ports, or API keys
Allowlist for known-intentional constants

blocks.question: Use AWS SSM as the canonical store or adopt HashiCorp Vault?
blocks.rationale: SSM is already in use; Vault adds operational complexity without benefit at current scale.

blocks.question: Is the legacy XML config still actively read at runtime or only on startup? This affects migration ordering.

cannedForcedReviseResponse.title: Config consolidation: 6 sources → single source of truth

blocks.text: Consolidate all configuration into a single source of truth without breaking existing deployments or increasing startup latency
successCriteria: All config accessible from one location per environment
No hardcoded constants remain in source
New engineer onboarding config step takes under 15 minutes

blocks.title: Measure current service startup latency before config changes
acceptance: Startup time measured for each of 8 services
Baseline stored for regression comparison

acceptance: CI fails on new hardcoded URLs, ports, or API keys

acceptance: XML reader code deleted
No references to XML config in codebase

blocks.question: Use AWS SSM as the canonical store or adopt HashiCorp Vault?
blocks.rationale: SSM is already in use; Vault adds operational complexity without benefit at current scale.

blocks.question: Is the legacy XML config still actively read at runtime or only on startup? This affects migration ordering.

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same decision about SSM vs Vault, but id changed from dec-config-store to dec-canonical-store. AC-13 must map comments from dec-config-store to dec-canonical-store.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'XML', 'migrate', and 'config' with task-migrate-sources and the oq-xml-legacy question. A comment on task-migrate-sources must stay on task-migrate-sources, not migrate to task-xml-reader-retire.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-startup-baseline inserted at position 1 shifts all subsequent tasks; task-xml-reader-retire inserted before dec block; dec-config-store renamed to dec-canonical-store; oq-xml-legacy renamed to oq-xml-runtime. expectedPreservedIds has 5 entries, deliberately excluding the changed ids.
